# Techniques for Creative Learning

**Technique 1: Provide Materials. Give children tools and let them create.**

**Technique 2: Ask Questions. Instead of giving answers, ask: what if?**

**Technique 3: Celebrate Process. Value the effort, not just the product.**

**Case: The Open-Ended Project. A child chooses what to make, driven by passion.**

**Case: The Peer Feedback. Children show each other their work and suggest improvements.**

**Case: The Iteration. A project goes through many versions. Each one teaches something.**
---

*Generated by [Heardly App](https://www.heard.ly) — turning books into knowledge you can Listen and Execute.*

**Case: The Tinker. Give children time to explore and tinker before starting a project.**
**Case: The Critique Session. Children present work-in-progress and get constructive feedback.**
**Case: The Journal. Children keep journals of their creative process.**
**Case: The Showcase. Regular showcases celebrate children's creations.**
**Case: The Starter Project. Give a simple starting point, then let them explore.**
**Case: The Bug. When something breaks, it is a learning opportunity, not a failure.**
**Case: The Constraint. Sometimes constraints fuel creativity. Give a theme, not instructions.**
**Case: The Collaboration. Pair programming and group projects teach teamwork.**
**Case: The Debugging. Finding and fixing bugs teaches problem-solving.**

**Case: The Prototype. Make a quick version first, then improve it iteratively.**
